item.title: The ‘Broken Veteran’ Excuse
item.description: Graham Platner’s defenders are playing into a dangerous stereotype about Americans who have fought in war.
item.dc:creator: Mike Nelson

item.title: So Much for Leaving Abortion Up to the States
item.description: Louisiana’s case against the FDA is not just about one drug.
item.dc:creator: Adam Serwer

item.title: The Voters Who Believe That Trump Defends Their Values
item.description: Why calls to ‘save democracy’ don’t work.
item.dc:creator: Katy Osborn

item.title: Trump’s Gladiator Delusion
item.description: The president’s South Lawn fight lacks the ambition of ancient Rome.
item.dc:creator: Cullen Murphy

item.title: The Women Who Don’t Own Clothes
item.description: The rental service Nuuly is taking over America’s closets.
item.dc:creator: Annie Joy Williams

item.title: Americans Are Already Paying Dearly for the National Debt
item.description: A spendthrift government is raising borrowing costs for everyone.
item.dc:creator: Martha Gimbel

item.title: The Boeing 747 Begins Its Final Descent
item.description: The jet was perhaps the pinnacle of American engineering excellence. Its retirement signals an end to an era of American culture—and ambition.
item.dc:creator: Ian Bogost

item.title: Why60 MinutesShould Take Critiques of Its Work Seriously
item.description: Scott Pelley’s recent interview reveals why the show shouldn’t ignore the accusations of bias.
item.dc:creator: Conor Friedersdorf

item.title: Pete Hegseth, Cornball in Chief
item.description: The secretary of defense is a fountain of corny performances and rhetoric.
item.dc:creator: Tom Nichols
